mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positively correlated with month-over-month PAYEMS changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound constraint amplifies forward guidance effects and suppresses short-term discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following FEDFUNDS below 0.25 do not exceed unconditional 60-day baseline returns with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate a broad-based demand-supply expansion that lifts profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; either signal alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Instances where both RSAFS and INDPRO rise month-over-month do not produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the joint signal underperforms the individual RSAFS signal in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: In periods when the unemployment rate exceeds 6 percent, a subsequent month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a labor market recovery that reduces recession probability and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, month-over-month declines in UNRATE are not followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and forward earnings pressure, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative momentum in INDPRO over 3 months fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC magnitude below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.0 percent, a contemporaneous rise in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) generates a negative real interest rate environment that stimulates borrowing and equity inflows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of FEDFUNDS below 1.0% and rising CPIAUCSL does not produce positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 900 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and deteriorating household formation that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with HOUST below 900 thousand are not followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns, and the signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate demand expansion that flows into corporate sales and profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to exhibit positive rank correlation with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).
